name: docker
description: Container runtime. On macOS this means Docker Desktop (or colima); on Linux, Docker Engine.

installs.notes: Installs the Docker Desktop app. The user must launch it once (`open -a Docker`) before `docker` commands work; first launch asks for privileged-helper approval.
Lighter alternative: colima provides the Linux VM, `colima start` replaces Docker Desktop.
Distro package; simplest. Docker's own apt repo (docker-ce) is newer but requires adding a repo + GPG key — offer it only if the user needs current versions.
Installs Docker Desktop for Windows. Requires WSL 2 or Hyper-V. A restart may be needed, and Docker Desktop must be launched once before `docker` commands work.

commonFailures: `docker: command not found` right after Docker Desktop install on macOS — the app was never launched, so the CLI symlinks were never created.
`Cannot connect to the Docker daemon` — the daemon isn't running (Desktop/colima not started, or `sudo systemctl start docker` on Linux).

On Linux, `permission denied on /var/run/docker.sock` — the user isn't in the `docker` group; fix is `sudo usermod -aG docker $USER` plus a re-login, and that group grants root-equivalent access, which is worth saying out loud.
`docker: command not found` on Windows after Docker Desktop install — close and reopen the terminal, or ensure Docker Desktop is running.
